name: Discord
tagline: Community messaging — servers, channels, threads, bots.
security: Bot tokens grant full bot privileges — keep server-only.
Verify interaction signatures using Ed25519 + DISCORD_PUBLIC_KEY on every incoming HTTP interaction.
Use slash commands over message-content scanning when possible — fewer permissions needed.
Webhook URLs are themselves secrets — anyone with the URL can post.

patterns.name: Webhook notifier
patterns.description: Use a Discord webhook URL to post messages to a single channel — no bot needed.

patterns.name: Slash commands
patterns.description: Build /commands using interactions endpoint and respond with embeds.

patterns.name: Moderation bot
patterns.description: Subscribe to message events via gateway and apply automated actions.

triggers.name: Message received
triggers.description: Fires when a message is sent in a guild channel the bot can see.

triggers.name: Member joined server
triggers.description: Fires when a new user joins a guild.

triggers.name: Reaction added
triggers.description: Fires when an emoji reaction is added to a message.

triggers.name: Slash command invoked
triggers.description: Fires when a user invokes a registered slash command.

actions.name: Send message to channel
actions.description: Post a message to a Discord channel.

actions.name: Send direct message
actions.description: Open a DM channel with a user and send a message.

actions.name: Create channel
actions.description: Create a new channel in a guild.

actions.name: Assign role
actions.description: Add a role to a guild member.

actions.name: Post via webhook
actions.description: POST a message to a channel using its Webhook URL.

auth.description: Bot token used as Authorization: Bot <token> for the REST API and gateway.
Webhook URL embedding its own auth — use for low-risk one-way notifications.
OAuth 2.0 for installing the bot to user-owned guilds.
